01
Phase 1
Pain Points & Solutions
7 core modules  ·  Full screenshot demo  ·  Extra feature highlights

NPIC Current Operational Pain Points

01

Quality Inspection Data Severely Delayed

Test results are only compiled at shift end, preventing real-time mid-shift decisions. Hours-long information gap between drying completion and available results.

02

Packaging Scheduling Cannot Be Planned Until End-of-Shift Test Results

Packaging arrangements must wait until near shift end for all water activity results. Night shift scheduling is severely delayed, making it hard to pre-allocate packaging resources.

03

Dry Room Relies on Manual Paper Records

Every morning shift requires manual transcription of all cart intake forms, taking 3+ hours with frequent transcription errors causing data inaccuracies.

04

Sales Cannot Access Real-Time Production & Inventory Status

Systems are not integrated. Sales cannot track production progress or inventory in real time, lacking data to commit to delivery dates or plan stock accurately.

Phase 1 Core Workflow

Scan Work Order
Create Batch
→
Scan Into
Pending Dry Queue
→
Dry Room Check-In
Assign Dryer Slot
→
Due Reminder
→ Take Sample
→
Enter Water Activity
System Auto-Judges

✓ PASS — Qualified

Passed products immediately enter the packaging queue. Managers release with one click; packaging team sees the incoming plan in advance.

  • → Module E dashboard displays in real time
  • → Feeds into Phase 2 packaging flow

✗ FAIL — Non-Conforming

Instantly triggers the disposal workflow. Management receives real-time notification; decision made within the current shift.

  • Options: Retest / Re-dry / Room temp dry / Scrap
  • → Module D records full audit trail for complete quality traceability

Module A — Production Batch & Cart Management

A
Production Batch & Cart Management
  • Scan work order to create a production batch; system auto-generates cart numbers (WO-001 to WO-N)
  • Each cart label is linked to its system record; scan to query real-time status instantly
  • Scan to record production details and dry room check-in/check-out — completely replaces daily manual transcription (saves 3+ hours)
💡 Solves Pain Point 03: Manual transcription 3+ hours → Scan to complete, zero data delay

Module B — Dry Room Real-Time Monitoring

B
Dry Room Real-Time Monitoring
  • Real-time display of carts in each dry room and their status (Drying / Due / Pending Removal)
  • Auto-calculates estimated completion time for each cart based on the target drying duration
  • Auto-push due reminders to prevent over-drying or delayed removal; viewable on phone or computer
💡 Solves Pain Point 03: Paper check-in/check-out → Real-time system status with auto due reminders

Module C — Sampling & Inspection Flow

C
Sampling & Inspection Flow
  • System auto-recommends sampling batches per configured ratio (e.g., 1 sample per N carts), reducing unnecessary testing
  • After the inspector enters the water activity value, the system auto-judges Pass/Fail against each SKU's spec limits
  • Representative sampling: one cart tested per group, result auto-synced to all carts in the group — no per-cart waiting
💡 Solves Pain Point 01: End-of-shift report → Results visible immediately after testing, decision speed dramatically improved

Module D — Non-Conforming Disposal Workflow

D
Non-Conforming Disposal Workflow
  • Failed batches immediately trigger disposal; options: Retest / Re-dry / Room temp dry / Scrap — one click in system
  • Management receives real-time notification; Hold and disposal decisions fully digitally recorded for complete quality traceability
  • Disposal completed within the current shift, preventing batch accumulation to shift end and reducing impact on the next shift
💡 Solves Pain Points 01 & 02: Problem batches pile up at shift end → Real-time notification + disposed within shift

Module E — Management Dashboard

E
Management Dashboard
  • Real-time overview: carts drying / pending sampling / pending inspection / today's pass rate / batches needing attention — phone/tablet accessible
  • 3-day rolling drying plan forecast, enabling the packaging team to pre-schedule staff and reduce idle waiting time
  • Managers release passed products into the packaging queue with one click — fully remote, no on-site presence needed
💡 Solves Pain Point 02: Must wait for all end-of-shift results before scheduling → Real-time test visibility + 3-day forecast, plan packaging in advance

Module F — Full Batch Traceability

F
Full Batch Traceability
  • Any batch fully traceable: production lot → sub-lots (carts) → sampling records → inspection results → disposal decisions → release time
  • For interdepartmental disputes, the system provides a complete digital evidence chain — no paper records required
  • Supports multi-dimensional queries and export by work order number, batch number, date, SKU, and more
💡 Full quality audit trail: Any batch's inspection → disposal → release chain queryable at any time, with complete evidence

Module G — Product & Inspection Standard Management EXTRA

G
Product & Inspection Standard Mgmt EXTRA
  • Create products and customize test items with pass/fail ranges (dual-limit mode or upper-limit-only mode)
  • Set a manual intervention zone: boundary values reviewed by supervisor to avoid borderline judgment disputes
✦ Delivered beyond original proposal scope: Product profiles + visual inspection standard configuration — add new SKUs without code changes

Phase 1 Key Value Summary

⚡ Operational Efficiency
  • Scan replaces paper check-in/check-out; related operation time reduced by 50%+
  • System auto-recommends sampling batches, eliminating manual cart counting
  • Test data entered directly into the system, eliminating the "handwrite → re-enter to Excel" double-handling
  • Decision speed: end-of-shift report → visible immediately after test completion
🛡 Strengthened Quality Control
  • Non-conforming batches pushed in real time; managers complete disposal within the shift
  • Prevents problem batch accumulation to shift end, reducing impact on the next shift
  • Test anomalies sync to the production line in real time, enabling fast process adjustments
  • Builds a complete inspection history to support long-term quality trend analysis
🔗 Improved Cross-Department Collaboration
  • Planning, QC, and packaging teams share one unified real-time data platform
  • Full batch traceability — complete digital evidence for any interdepartmental dispute
  • Significantly reduces communication overhead and friction from information asymmetry
  • Management can oversee full production status remotely, without relying on layer-by-layer reporting
02
Phase 2
Full-Chain Digitization Vision
Packaging → Warehouse Tracking  ·  Production Records: Excel → Online

Phase 2 ①  Packaging → Warehouse: Replace Excel Manual Tracking

Current State — Paper + Excel
  • Packaging staff handwrite records, then manually enter into Excel after completion
  • Warehouse receiving records and packaging records are kept separately, consolidated only the next day
  • "Warehouse says shipped, packaging says not received" — a recurring conflict
  • Reconciliation disputes arise with no digital evidence to reference
  • Inventory data is delayed; sales cannot see finished goods stock in real time
→
Phase 2 Target — Fully Online System
  • Passed products auto-enter the packaging queue after release — no manual notification needed
  • Scan to confirm packaging completion; records sync in real time — goodbye to Excel manual entry
  • Finished goods intake records auto-generated; warehouse inventory updated in real time
  • Eliminates the "warehouse says shipped, packaging says not received" information black hole
  • Complete digital evidence chain; disputes resolvable in one click, inventory visible in real time

Phase 2 ②  Production Records Digitization: From Paper/Excel to Online System

Current State — Paper + Excel
  • Production staff output/hours rely on paper records, often illegible
  • Daily manual consolidation into Excel is time-consuming and error-prone
  • Historical data scattered across multiple files; difficult to query or trace
  • Completely disconnected from QC inspection data, preventing correlated analysis
  • No real-time visibility into current shift output; management relies on manual reporting
→
Phase 2 Target — Fully Online System
  • Production staff scan or enter output/hours on tablet; syncs in real time
  • All production records (work order / output / batch / shift) fully managed online
  • Auto-linked to Phase 1 QC data, forming a complete production-quality chain
  • One-click production report generation — no more manual Excel consolidation
  • Historical data queryable and analyzable, supporting production optimization decisions

Phase 2 Vision: Complete Digital Chain from "Production Plan" to "Customer Receipt"
